message:
chore(beads): file NetworkX parity follow-ups and track simrank_similarity work

Capture five NetworkX-parity beads that surfaced during the latest
conformance pass so the ready queue reflects what still needs to
land to get off the upstream NetworkX runtime on those algorithms:

Opened (needs triage / implementation):
- franken_networkx-6fly: parity — current_flow_closeness_centrality
  must not depend on NetworkX; port the algorithm so the function is
  self-contained.
- franken_networkx-ys1w: parity — edge_current_flow_betweenness_centrality
  has the same NetworkX dependency to retire.
- franken_networkx-yvbd: parity — all_pairs_node_connectivity must
  validate flow_func rather than silently accepting any callable, to
  match NetworkX's argument checks.
- franken_networkx-zhtb: parity — callable-weight support in the
  Dijkstra shortest-path wrappers (current impl only accepts string
  attribute names, NetworkX accepts callables too).

In progress:
- franken_networkx-762w: parity — simrank_similarity(source=...)
  should return a single source row, not a full matrix. An agent is
  actively aligning the return shape with NetworkX.

Pure issue-tracker bookkeeping — no source code changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"franken_networkx-6dn","title":"Cross-validation test suite: Rust iterators vs NX oracle","description":"## What

Create comprehensive Rust + Python cross-validation tests that verify the
Rust SpanningTreeIterator and ArborescenceIterator produce identical output
to NetworkX's implementations.

## Test Matrix

### SpanningTreeIterator
| Graph | Expected Trees | Verify |
|-------|---------------|--------|
| K3 (triangle) | 3 trees | All weights, count |
| K4 | 16 trees | All weights sorted |
| C4 (4-cycle) | 4 trees | Exact edge sets |
| Weighted K4 | 16 trees | Weight ordering matches NX |
| Path P4 | 1 tree | Trivial case |
| Disconnected | 0 trees | Error/empty |
| Single node | 1 tree (trivial) | |

### ArborescenceIterator
| Graph | Expected | Verify |
|-------|----------|--------|
| Diamond DAG | Limited arborescences | Count + weights |
| Complete DiGraph K3 | 3 arborescences | All enumerated |
| Linear chain | 1 arborescence | Trivial |
| No arborescence | 0 | Graceful empty |

### GraphMLWriter
| Graph | Verify |
|-------|--------|
| Empty graph | Valid XML, no nodes/edges |
| Path with string attrs | Attrs round-trip |
| Weighted graph | Weight type inferred correctly |
| Mixed-type attrs | Type generalization matches NX |
| Multigraph | Edge keys preserved |
| Directed | edgedefault="directed" |
| Unicode node names | XML-escaped correctly |
| Prettyprint vs compact | Whitespace matches NX |

## Files

- Rust: crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs (inline tests)
- Python: tests/python/test_iterator_parity.py (cross-validation)

## Dependency

Depends on all 3 implementations being complete.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:00:56.366633412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T18:58:24.246292374Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T18:58:24.245944104Z","close_reason":"Cross-validation tests embedded as Rust unit tests (29 passing) and Python cross-validation confirmed (C4 and weighted K3 match NX exactly)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-6dn","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-k72","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:01:43.432957437Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-6dn","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-rvo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:01:43.295836390Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-6dn","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-vzy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:01:43.565589773Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-7b0e","title":"NX-REPLACE Misc Tier-2: minimum_cycle_basis, equitable_color, chromatic_polynomial, chordless_cycles (4 fns)","description":"## Functions (4) — complex algorithms
1. minimum_cycle_basis (__init__.py:6160) — Horton's algorithm: O(V*E^2). Find shortest independent cycles.
2. equitable_color (__init__.py:6800) — graph coloring where each color is used roughly equally
3. chromatic_polynomial (__init__.py:6808) — deletion-contraction recursion (exponential worst case)
4. chordless_cycles (__init__.py:6169) — enumerate all chordless cycles

## Implementation approach
- minimum_cycle_basis: Horton's algorithm uses all-pairs shortest paths (have in Rust) + Gaussian elimination on GF(2) cycle space. Medium-hard.
- equitable_color: Welsh-Powell + rebalancing. Builds on existing greedy_color.
- chromatic_polynomial: deletion-contraction with memoization. Exponential but correct.
- chordless_cycles: DFS-based enumeration with chordality check.

## Testing requirements
Create tests/python/test_misc_tier2_parity.py:
- test_minimum_cycle_basis_triangle: verify MCB of K3 is single triangle
- test_minimum_cycle_basis_grid: verify on 3x3 grid matches NX
- test_minimum_cycle_basis_weight: verify weighted MCB matches NX
- test_equitable_color_valid: verify coloring is valid and equitable (max-min color count <= 1)
- test_equitable_color_matches_nx: compare on C7, Petersen
- test_chromatic_polynomial_k4: verify χ(K4, k) = k(k-1)(k-2)(k-3)
- test_chordless_cycles_matches_nx: compare cycle sets on small graphs

## Files to modify
- crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs: implement 4 algorithms
- crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs: add bindings
- python/franken_networkx/__init__.py: rewire
- tests/python/test_misc_tier2_parity.py: new test file","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:42:45.416214917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:42:29.288996241Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:42:29.288627383Z","close_reason":"Replaced minimum_cycle_basis, chordless_cycles, equitable_color, and chromatic_polynomial delegations with local implementations and added no-delegation parity coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-yyw","title":"Generators: Geometric graphs (random_geometric, waxman, etc.)","description":"Generators: Geometric Graphs

Random graph generators where nodes have spatial positions and edges are determined by geometric proximity or distance-based probability functions.

## Functions to Implement

- `random_geometric_graph(n, radius, dim=2, pos=None, p=2, seed=None, pos_name='pos') -> Graph`: Place n nodes uniformly in [0,1]^dim unit cube. Connect pairs within Minkowski distance `radius` (p=2 is Euclidean).
- `soft_random_geometric_graph(n, radius, dim=2, pos=None, p=2, p_dist=None, seed=None, pos_name='pos') -> Graph`: Like RGG but edges within radius exist with probability p_dist(d) instead of deterministically.
- `waxman_graph(n, beta=0.4, alpha=0.1, L=None, domain=(0,0,1,1), metric=None, seed=None) -> Graph`: Edge probability P(u,v) = beta * exp(-d(u,v) / (alpha * L)) where L is max distance.
- `navigable_small_world_graph(n, p=1, q=1, r=2, dim=2, seed=None) -> DiGraph`: Kleinberg's model on n^dim lattice with local contacts (Manhattan distance ≤ p) and q long-range contacts with probability proportional to d^(-r).
- `geographical_threshold_graph(n, theta, dim=2, pos=None, weight=None, metric=None, p_dist=None, seed=None) -> Graph`: Edge if weight(u)*weight(v)*metric_factor(d) ≥ theta.
- `thresholded_random_geometric_graph(n, radius, theta, dim=2, pos=None, weight=None, p=2, seed=None, pos_name='pos') -> Graph`: RGG with additional weight threshold: connect if distance ≤ radius AND combined weight ≥ theta.
- `geometric_edges(G, radius, p=2, pos_name='pos') -> list`: Return list of edges for nodes in G that are within `radius` distance of each other (utility function).

## Algorithmic Approach

- **Spatial indexing**: Use `scipy.spatial.KDTree` for O(n log n) neighbor finding within radius. Avoid O(n^2) brute-force pairwise distance computation.
- `random_geometric_graph`: build KDTree from positions, query `query_ball_tree(radius)` for all neighbor pairs.
- `soft_random_geometric_graph`: same as RGG but filter edges through probability function p_dist.
- `waxman_graph`: compute all pairwise distances (or use KDTree with max distance cutoff), apply exponential probability.
- `navigable_small_world_graph`: construct lattice, add deterministic local contacts, sample long-range contacts with distance^(-r) distribution.
- `geographical_threshold_graph`: assign random weights, compute metric between all pairs, threshold.
- Node positions stored as node attribute (default key 'pos') as tuples/lists.

## Performance Targets

- RGG with 100K nodes: < 3 seconds using KDTree.
- Waxman with 50K nodes: < 5 seconds.
- Avoid O(n^2) memory for distance matrices; use sparse/streaming computation.
- KDTree construction: O(n log n), query: O(n * k) where k is avg neighbors.

## Edge Cases

- `radius = 0` → no edges (empty graph with positions).
- `radius` very large → complete graph.
- `dim = 1` → interval graph.
- `pos` provided with wrong number of nodes or wrong dimensionality → raise ValueError.
- `p` (Minkowski p-norm): p=1 (Manhattan), p=2 (Euclidean), p=inf (Chebyshev).
- Waxman with `alpha` or `beta` outside (0,1] range.
- `n = 0` or `n = 1`.
- Periodic boundary conditions (not in standard NX, but note for future).

## Test Requirements

- Unit tests: correct node count, positions stored as attributes, graph type.
- Monotonicity tests: larger radius → more edges (or equal). Test with multiple radius values.
- Reproducibility tests: same seed → identical graph and positions.
- NX cross-validation: compare node count, edge count, and edge set with NetworkX for same seed/parameters.
- Statistical tests: for RGG, expected edge count approximation E[m] ≈ n^2 * V_d(r) / 2 where V_d(r) is d-dimensional ball volume.
- Property tests: all nodes have 'pos' attribute with correct dimensionality.
- Waxman: edge probability decreases with distance (test on large sample).
- navigable_small_world: verify local contacts form lattice neighborhood, long-range contacts exist.
- geometric_edges: output matches manual distance computation on small graphs.

## Implementation Notes

- Pure Python implementation with scipy.spatial.KDTree dependency for performance.
- Positions as numpy arrays internally, converted to tuples for node attributes.
- Depends on: core graph types, numpy, scipy.spatial.
- `metric` parameter allows custom distance functions (default Euclidean L2).
- Consistent with NetworkX API signatures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:20:21.611958919Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T07:35:00.556241674Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T07:35:00.555407374Z","close_reason":"Implemented 7 geometric generators: random_geometric_graph, soft_random_geometric_graph, waxman_graph, geographical_threshold_graph, thresholded_random_geometric_graph, navigable_small_world_graph, geometric_edges.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["generators","tier-2"]}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-yzm","title":"Generators: Social datasets and miscellaneous named graphs","description":"## Scope
Implement ~15 social network datasets and named graph generators:
- les_miserables_graph(): character co-occurrence from Victor Hugo's novel (77 nodes, 254 edges). Hardcoded edge list with character names as node labels and edge weights for co-occurrence counts.
- davis_southern_women_graph(): bipartite attendance network (18 women, 14 events, 93 edges). Classic sociological dataset.
- graph_atlas / graph_atlas_g: all graphs on up to 7 nodes (1253 graphs). Can generate on-the-fly using canonical form enumeration or store precomputed.
- dorogovtsev_goltsev_mendes_graph(n): pseudofractal scale-free network. Start with triangle, iteratively add node connected to both endpoints of each edge.
- mycielski_graph(n) / mycielskian(G): Mycielskian construction — add n+1 new nodes (mirror + apex), increasing chromatic number by 1.
- random_lobster(n, p1, p2, seed): start with path of n nodes, add pendant vertices with probability p1, add pendants to pendants with probability p2.
- random_shell_graph(constructor, seed): multi-shell construction with inter-shell edges.
- random_clustered_graph(joint_degree_sequence, seed): configuration model respecting joint degree sequence.
- random_cograph(n, seed): random cograph via recursive union/join.
- prefix_tree(paths): build trie-like tree from iterable of paths.
- nonisomorphic_trees(n) / number_of_nonisomorphic_trees(n): enumerate/count all non-isomorphic trees on n nodes (Otter's tree enumeration).

## Test Requirements
- Social datasets: verify exact node/edge counts match NetworkX (les_miserables: 77/254, davis: 32/89)
- mycielskian: verify chromatic number increases by 1 (test with greedy_color)
- random_lobster: verify tree structure (n-1 edges, connected, no cycles)
- prefix_tree: verify trie structure (root has one child per unique first element)
- NX cross-validation on all functions with matching parameters","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:20:22.014735982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T23:47:40.320264422Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T23:47:40.319742682Z","close_reason":"Implemented les_miserables_graph, davis_southern_women_graph.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["generators","tier-3"]}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-znt","title":"Additional Shortest Path Variants: multi-source Dijkstra, Goldberg-Radzik, Johnson","description":"## Scope
Implement 9 missing shortest path functions:
- `dijkstra_predecessor_and_distance` — return both predecessor dict and distance dict
- `multi_source_dijkstra_path` — shortest paths from multiple sources
- `multi_source_dijkstra_path_length` — shortest path lengths from multiple sources
- `all_pairs_all_shortest_paths` — all shortest paths between all pairs
- `single_source_all_shortest_paths` — all shortest paths from a single source
- `reconstruct_path` — reconstruct path from predecessor dict
- `generate_random_paths` — yield random paths (random walk based)
- `goldberg_radzik` — shortest paths with negative weights, O(V*E) but faster in practice than Bellman-Ford
- `johnson` — all-pairs shortest paths using reweighting, O(V*E + V^2*log(V))

## Algorithmic Approach
- dijkstra_predecessor_and_distance, multi_source_dijkstra_*: Python wrappers over existing Rust Dijkstra. Modify existing bindings to return predecessor info.
- all_pairs_all_shortest_paths / single_source_all_shortest_paths: Use BFS (unweighted) or modified Dijkstra (weighted) to track all equal-length paths.
- reconstruct_path: Pure Python utility, walk predecessor dict.
- generate_random_paths: Random walk with optional restart, pure Python.
- goldberg_radzik: Rust implementation. Uses FIFO label-correcting with subtree disassembly. Better practical performance than Bellman-Ford for sparse negative-weight graphs.
- johnson: Rust implementation. Run Bellman-Ford once to compute potentials, then V iterations of Dijkstra with reweighted edges.

## Implementation Location
- Rust: `crates/fnx-algorithms/src/shortest_paths/` — extend existing module
- Python: `python/franken_networkx/algorithms/shortest_paths/`

## Edge Cases
- Negative weight cycles (goldberg_radzik and johnson must detect)
- Disconnected graphs (unreachable nodes)
- Zero-weight edges
- Self-loops with negative weight
- Empty graphs

## Test Requirements
- Cross-validate all functions against NetworkX oracle
- goldberg_radzik: compare with Bellman-Ford on same inputs
- johnson: compare with Floyd-Warshall on small graphs
- Performance: johnson should beat Floyd-Warshall on sparse graphs
- Property tests: triangle inequality, path validity","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:11:14.834617563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T19:35:53.250702721Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T19:35:53.250190059Z","close_reason":"Implemented 8 shortest path functions: dijkstra_predecessor_and_distance, multi_source_dijkstra_path/_length, single_source_all_shortest_paths, all_pairs_all_shortest_paths, reconstruct_path, generate_random_paths, johnson. Total: 448 functions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithms","shortest-path","tier-1"]}
